summary:
Pyrefly, the new type checker from Meta, helped boost NumPy's type-completeness from 33% to nearly 90%. They started by identifying that a main issue was partial type information on core classes like `ndarray`, which was 98% complete but still flagged. Fixing a single annotation typo in a method resolved this, instantly doubling coverage to over 80%.

Then, attention turned to `MaskedArray`, which had many methods missing return type annotations. Adding these pushed coverage further. The remaining gaps were in top-level `numpy.ma` functions and some overloads that needed refinement. The team also integrated Pyright into NumPy's CI to prevent regressions.

This effort shows how strategic, small fixes can dramatically improve type coverage in large codebases, enhancing the developer experience across the ecosystem.<｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>
